meta.title: How Hardware Wallets and Multi-Chain Swaps Power Internet Capital Markets

transcript: Oh wow, such a big stage, isn't it? I'm very excited to be here guys. Let's go. Wait, where's my teammates? Okay, let's begin. Well my name is Louise, I'm the CEO and Co-Founder at Ryder. Yes, there. Before we begin, I want to tell you a little bit of backstory. So, exactly two years ago at Breakpoint, we actually unveiled Ryder One, the first prototype. And we actually launched our first crowdfunding campaign as well. It was an idea back then. And big shout out to Austin Federa, whom we met at Japan. He was actually the guy who brought us to Solana. Solana opened a lot of opportunities for us, and it opened a whole new door. And this is us now. And I'm very proud to say that on behalf of our team, we're a global company that ships thousands of units generating revenue. Now, why are we doing Ryder? We started Ryder because we wanted to solve the biggest bottleneck of self-custody. I mean, it's not because crypto is broken, it's because... we're... not broken because... if... It's not because crypto was broken, but it's because of the experience. So let's go over it one by one, right? So as a user, the current experience for me is intimidating. I mean, who here actually loves their hardware wallet? Come on guys, I need energy in the room. Yes, let's go. That gives me energy as well. So nobody... I mean, I had eight hardware wallets and I didn't like it, right? There's too many steps, there's too many jargon, there's too much room for errors. And they also ask us to write these 12 or 24 words, which feels like very wrong for 2025. They tell you to hide it, protect it, don't lose it. And we all know the horror stories that come with it, right? So at Ryder we try to solve this issue. We actually team up... Okay, we always ask ourselves, okay, what if we... we rebuild self-custody with zero assumptions? What if we build a product on how great products are being built? You know? So with great user feedback from people like you and me, with real conviction and a whole lot of obsession. So here, I'm gonna show you on how we actually obsess every night on user onboarding until like every friction point comes... is removed, until a beginner like you could get onboarded in less than 60 seconds. And our UI/UX lead Quentin can actually tell you on how we fight over the button placements until 3:00 AM in the morning. And the same kind of obsession can be seen on our firmware and security. Our CTO Marvin over there actually created TapSafe Recovery, which makes recovery simple, safer, and flexible, instead of being fragile. And again, the same obsession can be seen on our product and manufacturing line. You know, Julian, our CPO, can... he can also tell you how we went on 23 mechanical iterations so you can have a product that you can hold in your hand and you would want to carry. And today, I'm gonna introduce you to Ryder One. It's actually a hardware wallet without the hard part, you know? We make it impossible to lose your wallet. And what a better way for me to... actually show it than... to show the whole process. So Ryder One, you only need a few taps. You tap to create and backup your wallet, you tap to send, and you tap to recover. So let's go on the process. So as you can see here, you simply tap to pair, and you also tap to create and backup your wallet. It takes less than 60 seconds. Let's go. And the next thing that you do is you want to verify the transaction that you initiated on your mobile app on a screen. As you can see here, he's verifying it. And on the sides, you can see the infinity edge lighting up that you can see with the secure LEDs that's performing on... when there's a secure operation. Now, you know that displays are very important when it comes to a hardware wallet because you don't really know what you're signing. So a really good example is, will you pay your credit card on a POS terminal that doesn't have a screen? That's why we have a saying in our space: You don't trust, you verify. Next, so Ryder also create... enables you to create backups whenever you want it, either through Recovery Tags or Recovery Contacts. Yeah, and I'm gonna give you some of my favorite testimonials ever since we launched the product. And the first one is from Uncle Tim. I don't think he know... he knows it that I call him Uncle, I think it's only me. So this is one of the testimonials that we have. So after showing you on how we perfected onboarding, transaction sending, and recovery, you're probably now wondering, okay, what's next? So since we're in Solana and talking about Internet Capital Markets... he seems very like sad, you know? Internet Capital Markets... of course, the next is... okay, let's go there, right? So you... as users and institutions, you operate on multi-chain markets. But there's three problems. So multi-chain markets, first one is, okay, the... there's fragmented liquidity. Next one is, as a user, you want the best execution safely. And the third thing... this is the slide for it... and the third thing is software wallets are quite dangerous and risky. So in order to access a multi... multi... In order to access a... multi-chain market, all you need is two things. First is on the hardware layer, you want to protect every action at its source. And the next one is a multi-chain swaps that is very efficient. And today, I'm proud to announce that Ryder actually is gonna have it, you know? So multi-chain swaps is gonna be directly built-in into the Ryder One device. Fast, safe, and secure. It's gonna be powered by Wormhole, which can actually unlock deep liquidity. And this is gonna come in 2025. Now, before I end my presentation, of course I have a CTA towards the end for all the viewers on X, right? So before I... I end my presentation, I just wanna say that self-custody can be beautiful, it can be actually human, it can also be powerful without being painful, right? And at Ryder, we wanna give you control without the complexity, without the fear, and of course without the paper. Cause who... who ever... I mean, I don't think anybody uses paper and pen anymore, right? Do you? See, you don't. I think that's my presentation guys. A bit short, but I hope you like it. Thank you so much.

tags.impact_reason: Ryder One hardware wallet aims to improve self-custody, a foundational aspect of the Solana ecosystem, but the impact is moderate as it's one of many hardware wallet options.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose the Ryder One hardware wallet as the Semantic Anchor because it's the central product. The story, however, is about its transformation from inert storage ('coffin') into an active 'liquidity terminal' via Wormhole. Therefore, the visual concept shows the wallet being activated by vibrant, flowing data streams representing this cross-chain connection. For the homepage, I integrated the text 'SWAP' as a holographic projection from the device's screen. This is diegetic and directly communicates the wallet's new, primary function discussed in the headline.
visual_idea.description: A sleek, dark brushed-titanium Ryder One hardware wallet levitates against a deep, abstract background. Vibrant, multi-colored strands of light, representing the Wormhole connection, flow like plasma, piercing through the device and connecting it to a vast, out-of-focus network of light grids behind it. From the wallet's screen, a crisp, minimalist holographic UI projects forward, displaying the word 'SWAP' in glowing cyan letters, as if confirming a transaction. The lighting is dramatic, with sharp specular highlights on the metal and a soft glow emanating from the data streams.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A sleek, dark brushed-titanium Ryder One hardware wallet levitates majestically, appearing monumental against a deep, abstract cosmic void. Vibrant, multi-colored strands of bioluminescent plasma, representing the Wormhole connection, flow dynamically, piercing through the device with an ethereal glow and connecting it to a vast, out-of-focus network of intricate light grids behind it, emphasizing its role as 'The Conduit'. From the wallet's screen, a crisp, minimalist holographic UI projects forward, displaying the word 'SWAP' in glowing cyan letters, rendered with perfect clarity, as if confirming a vital transaction. The scene is bathed in dramatic, high-contrast, low-key cinematic product lighting, casting sharp specular highlights on the dark brushed-titanium surface and a soft, captivating glow emanating from the bioluminescent data streams. Volumetric light effects enhance the depth and atmosphere. Composed for a vertical 3:4 aspect ratio, with a slightly low-angle perspective, emphasizing the wallet's epic scale and leaving ample negative space towards the top, drawing the eye upwards. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra quality, ZBrush.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose the Ryder One hardware wallet as the Semantic Anchor because it's the central product. The story, however, is about its transformation from inert storage ('coffin') into an active 'liquidity terminal' via Wormhole. Therefore, the visual concept shows the wallet being activated by vibrant, flowing data streams representing this cross-chain connection. For the homepage, I integrated the text 'SWAP' as a holographic projection from the device's screen. This is diegetic and directly communicates the wallet's new, primary function discussed in the headline.
visual_idea.description: A macro shot focusing on the point where ethereal, glowing tendrils of multi-colored light phase directly through the solid, textured surface of the brushed titanium hardware wallet. The image highlights the material contrast: the cool, engineered precision of the metal case against the living, fluid energy of the data stream passing through it. Intricate refractions and caustics are visible where the light interacts with the metal's surface, creating a sense of powerful, frictionless transfer.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro shot, 1:1 square aspect ratio, focused centrally on the exact point where ethereal, glowing tendrils of vibrant, multi-colored bioluminescent plasma phase directly through the solid, finely textured surface of a brushed titanium hardware wallet. The composition emphasizes extreme detail and symmetry, filling the entire frame with the intricate material contrast: the cool, engineered precision and subtle reflective properties of the brushed metal case against the living, fluid, intensely energetic data stream passing through it. Intricate, crystalline refractions and stunning caustics are visibly dancing and interacting where the multi-colored light tendrils meet and penetrate the metal's surface, creating a powerful sense of frictionless transfer and deep dimensionality. Cinematic product lighting highlights the metal's texture with soft global illumination and a cool, subtle rim light, contrasting dramatically with the intense, volumetric glow of the plasma, which casts vibrant, ethereal light and shadows. The atmosphere is clean and studio-like, allowing the materials and light interaction to dominate. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, physically based rendering (PBR), volumetric lighting, global illumination, macro lens, deep depth of field.

editorial.headline: Ryder Taps Wormhole to Turn Hardware Wallets into Liquidity Terminals
editorial.standfirst: The era of 'coffin' storage ends as Ryder integrates native cross-chain swaps. The move positions self-custody devices as active execution layers for Internet Capital Markets.
key_takeaways: Strategic Partnership: Ryder One will integrate **Wormhole** to enable native multi-chain swaps directly on-device.
Market Pivot: Moving beyond passive 'cold storage' to active participation in fragmented liquidity markets without software wallet risks.
Traction Update: Thousands of units now shipping globally following 23 mechanical iterations and a seedless 'TapSafe' architecture.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
Hardware wallets have historically functioned as vaults—secure, but stagnant 'coffins' for capital. At Solana Breakpoint 2025, **Ryder** CEO Louise Ivan Valencia Payawal challenged this paradigm, positioning the **Ryder One** not merely as a storage device, but as an active terminal for 'Internet Capital Markets.' The keynote’s central reveal—a partnership with interoperability protocol **Wormhole**—signals a critical shift: the ability to execute cross-chain swaps directly from hardware, bypassing the security vulnerabilities inherent in browser-based software wallets.

For institutional investors and high-volume traders, this integration addresses the 'execution vs. security' trade-off. By embedding **Wormhole**'s liquidity infrastructure into the device firmware, Ryder allows users to access fragmented markets across chains without exposing private keys to an online environment. This 'hardware layer protection' ensures that actions are verified at the source, effectively turning the device into a secure edge node for decentralized finance rather than just a passive holding pen.

Underpinning this new functionality is Ryder’s proprietary 'TapSafe' recovery system, which replaces the fragile 24-word seed phrase with Shamir Secret Sharing and NFC technology. By combining this user-friendly security architecture with the new active liquidity features, Ryder is explicitly targeting the bottleneck of self-custody: the friction of onboarding and the fear of execution errors. With thousands of units now generating revenue, the company is betting that the future of custody is not just about holding assets, but about deploying them safely.
